summary: M.Sc. Bioinformatics candidate at the University of Crete (with FORTH and HCMR), combining hands-on wet-lab experience in cancer genetics and pharmacogenomics with a growing command of Python, machine learning, and applied AI. Trilingual, self-driven learner, ReGeneration 2025 finalist, eager to contribute to AI/data, biotech, or computational biology teams.

education.institution: University of Crete (in partnership with FORTH and HCMR)
details: Coursework: Linear Algebra, Probability & Statistics, Python, Linux, Biomedical Databases, Molecular Biology & Genetics
Hands-on projects in dimensionality reduction (custom PCA via eigendecomposition and EM algorithm), statistical learning, and reproducible analysis pipelines on Linux/HPC environments

education.thesis: Genetic variants in the EPHX1 gene and susceptibility to Schizophrenia & Major Depressive Disorder

experience.title: Research Intern
experience.organization: NCSR "Demokritos" — Cancer Genetics & Hereditary Cancer Lab
highlights: Analyzed Sanger sequencing chromatograms to identify clinically relevant germline variants in hereditary cancer syndromes (BRCA1/2, Lynch syndrome)
Built detailed patient pedigrees and curated multi-generational family medical histories, directly supporting genetic counselling decisions
Collaborated with clinicians and geneticists in a multidisciplinary research environment, contributing to an ongoing hereditary cancer registry

experience.title: Undergraduate Researcher (Thesis)
experience.organization: University of Patras — Lab of Pharmacogenomics & Individualized Therapy (Prof. G. Patrinos)
highlights: Designed and executed an end-to-end genotyping pipeline: DNA extraction, PCR, RFLP, gel electrophoresis, and Sanger sequencing on patient cohorts
Identified EPHX1 variants and conducted a genetic association study linking them to Schizophrenia and Major Depressive Disorder susceptibility
Authored a full scientific report synthesizing experimental results with the pharmacogenomics literature, presented to the departmental research group

projects.name: Personal AI Agent for Automated Job Applications
highlights: Designing and building an end-to-end agentic AI system that ingests a stored candidate profile and generates tailored CVs and cover letters per job description, using LLM orchestration and structured prompting
Implementing modular pipeline (profile parser, JD analyzer, document generator) with the Claude API; integrating evaluation loop to score output relevance and ATS-friendliness
Demonstrates practical use of Generative AI services (Claude / OpenAI) and prompt engineering for real, measurable productivity gains

projects.name: Deep Learning Classifier for Genomic Variant Pathogenicity
highlights: Designing a supervised deep learning pipeline in PyTorch to classify clinical variants (pathogenic / benign) using annotated features from public genomic databases (ClinVar, gnomAD)

End-to-end workflow: data preprocessing and feature engineering, model training (MLP / 1D-CNN), evaluation with stratified cross-validation, and benchmarking against classical ML baselines (Random Forest, XGBoost)
Bridges domain expertise in cancer genetics with modern deep learning — a directly transferable methodology to AI-driven clinical decision support

awards_and_development: ReGeneration Finalist (20th Cycle, 2025) — selected among the top applicants nationwide for Greece's leading graduate career accelerator
Presenter at the 73rd National Conference of the Hellenic Society of Biochemistry & Molecular Biology (Athens, 2023)
Continuous learning: "Elements of AI" (Univ. of Helsinki, 2025), Bioinformatics Seminar (NTUA, 2024-25), Machine Learning A-Z (2025-present)
Certified Web & Mobile App Specialist (ICT Sector, 2023)
Active private tutor in Biology, Physics & Chemistry for high school and university students
